Identity.Name: StingMRIZones
Identity.Definition: MRI safety zone classification per ACR (American College of Radiology) MR Safe Practices. Drives MRI-suite spatial validation, the 5 Gauss line on plans, ferromagnetic-material exclusion in Zone IV, controlled access regimes, and shielding takedown.

Governance.StandardsBasis: ACR Guidance Document on MR Safe Practices: 2020; MHRA DB 2021(03) (UK); IEC 60601-2-33

Value.Definition: Zone I — freely accessible to general public. No screening required. Outside the MR-controlled environment.
Zone II — interface between freely-accessible public and controlled MR areas. Patients screened here; reception, changing rooms.
Zone III — restricted to MR-trained personnel + screened patients. Control room, magnet-adjacent corridors. Ferromagnetic objects prohibited.
Zone IV — inside the MR magnet room. Strictly MR-safe and MR-compatible materials only. Beyond the 5 Gauss line. Access requires zonal supervision.
